name: Structure that links the two cerebral hemispheres
question_text: Which structure is the large bundle of nerve fibres connecting the left and right cerebral hemispheres of the human brain?
general_feedback: The corpus callosum is a thick band of approximately 200 million axons that allows the left and right hemispheres to exchange information.
correct_feedback: Correct — The corpus callosum is the primary inter-hemispheric commissure.

answers.feedback: The corpus callosum is the primary inter-hemispheric commissure.
The cerebellum coordinates movement; it sits beneath the cerebrum and is not the link between hemispheres.
The medulla is part of the brainstem and regulates autonomic functions, not inter-hemispheric communication.
The hypothalamus regulates hormones and homeostasis; it is not the hemisphere-linking structure.
